items.caption: A Day on the Water — June 13, 2026
items.transcript: A day on the water with BlindSail, San Francisco Bay, June thirteenth. Morning at the Oakland Yacht Club. An all-volunteer crew, ready to cast off. Heading out past the Port of Oakland cranes. Lines off, and we are underway. Hands on the sheet, hauling it in. And pure joy on the water. Steering the bay as the ferries slide past. Right under the bridge — thumbs up. Sailing home along the span.

items.alt: Instructor Bree, in sunglasses and a life vest, leans in and talks with two sailors seated in the cockpit; one grips a blue-and-white line, the marina and water behind them.
items.caption: Bree walks us through the next move, line in hand, the Bay wide open behind us.

items.alt: David, an older sailor in a yellow life vest and green cap, sits at the tiller of a sailboat, looking out over the water toward the Oakland shoreline.
items.caption: David takes the tiller and settles in, eyes on the water ahead.

items.alt: Gayle, in a red life vest and sailing gloves, beams and gives a thumbs-up while sailing directly beneath the pale span of the Bay Bridge.
items.caption: Thumbs up from Gayle as we slip right under the bridge.

items.alt: Three women in life vests and sun hats sit together in a sailboat cockpit, smiling toward the camera, a marina full of masts behind them.
items.caption: All smiles before we cast off, crew and instructor ready to go.

items.alt: Instructor Colin sits between two sailors in the cockpit of a sailboat on open water, all three smiling, one holding a line overhead, green hills across the bay.
items.caption: Out on the water with Colin and the crew, everyone grinning.

items.alt: Instructor David, in a blue BlindSail top and life vest, sits on the bow smiling, the towering white cranes of the Port of Oakland rising behind him.
items.caption: David up on the bow as we head out past the Port of Oakland cranes.

items.alt: A close selfie of volunteer Jamie smiling in the foreground, with sailor David in a yellow vest and another crew member seated behind her aboard the boat.
items.caption: Jamie grabs a selfie with the crew as we get underway.

items.alt: Joelle laughs joyfully while holding coils of the main sheet line in gloved hands, a marina of masts behind her.
items.caption: Joelle works the main sheet and cannot stop laughing.

items.alt: Karen, in a BlindSail cap and life vest, laughs with her mouth open wide as she hauls on a line, sailing on open water.
items.caption: Pure joy from Karen, hauling in the sheet under full sun.

items.alt: Mary and Karen sit close together in the cockpit, both laughing, with the tall suspension tower of the Bay Bridge rising behind them.
items.caption: Mary and Karen soaking it up as we sail past the bridge tower.

items.alt: A low-angle view of the two-story Oakland Yacht Club building, its name in white letters across the front, with sailors gathered near the entrance.
items.caption: Where every sail begins, the Oakland Yacht Club.

items.alt: Sylvana sits at the tiller in a blue-and-yellow life vest, smiling, while instructor David sits nearby; a white racing sailboat passes behind them.
items.caption: Sylvana takes the helm, David riding along as backup.

items.alt: Sylvana laughs with her whole face while steering, a blue-and-green passenger ferry and Port of Oakland cranes on the water behind her.
items.caption: Sylvana at the helm, laughing, as a ferry slides by behind us.

items.alt: A wide view across the Oakland marina from the docks: rows of moored sailboats and yachts, calm water, and the green Oakland hills in the distance.
items.caption: Home waters, the slips at Oakland with the hills beyond.
David Cook shares what the day on the water meant to him.
items.transcript: What a team? Yes. And The confidence I felt was so magnificent. You know, I can get anxious. Sometimes you know I don't know if other people do that but I do it. And then yeah, it was, it was terrific. Day adventure. Will I do it again for sure? Yeah, yeah.

items.caption: Priscilla Aguiar reflects on her time at the helm.
items.transcript: And I said, okay, it was so fun. Nice so well done. And you think you come back? Absolutely love to hear that? Yes. Oh

items.caption: Lines off — pulling away from the slip with a full crew aboard.
A slow look across the slips before we head out.
